text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off in chips, rotation into defensives. Profit-taking crushed semiconductors and AI infrastructure after an 80.53% SOXX ytd run, while healthcare, utilities, and staples caught strong inflows as traders leaned into defensive carry and rate-cut expectations.[5][3] Cross-asset pricing in silver, gold, and credit plus a record-making DIA close signal a controlled de-risking out of crowded large-cap tech rather than broad panic.[2][4]

INDICES — VIX 16.1, below its 20d MA 18.2 and drifting lower.[VIX] Today’s return rank: SLV +2.69%, GLD +2.03%, DIA +1.05%, RSP +0.70%, SPY -0.13%, IJH -0.46%, IWM -0.58%, IJR -0.72%, QQQ -1.73%, SOXX -5.57%.[INDICES] STYLE: LARGE > SMALL by 0.45pp, highlighting pressure in small caps as tech and semis unwind.[STYLE] Most notable cross-asset move was SLV +2.69% alongside GLD +2.03%, reversing recent 20d losses of -19.08% and -8.21% and signaling renewed bid for hard-asset hedges.[CROSS-ASSET] SPY and QQQ remain above their 200d MAs, so no CTA sell trigger is active.[SPY][QQQ]

VOLUME — SOXX 1.4x, QQQ 1.3x, and RSP 1.2x volume show elevated attention to semis and broader equities, with SPY near average at ~1.0x and QQQ’s higher ratio confirming active de-risking in growth.[VOLUME BREADTH] SOXX down -5.57% on 1.4x and QQQ -1.73% on 1.3x flag distribution in chips and large-cap tech, while DIA 0.9x and SLV 0.7x suggest today’s Dow strength and silver pop came on relatively muted conviction.[INDICES][CROSS-ASSET]

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLV Health Care +2.63%, XLU Utilities +2.21%, XLP Staples +2.03%, XLB Materials +1.94%, XLF Financials +1.53%, XLRE Real Estate +1.13%, XLE Energy +0.78%, XLI Industrials +0.30%, XLC Comms -0.13%, XLY Discretionary -0.82%, XLK Technology -2.71%.[SECTORS] Top industries: GDX +4.48%, IHI +3.80%, SILJ +3.16%, IBB +2.93%; bottom: SMH -4.54%, SOXX -5.57%, DRAM -7.94%, WGMI -9.49%.[INDUSTRIES] Money clearly rotated into healthcare, utilities, staples, and gold miners, funded by aggressive selling in semiconductors, memory, and crypto-linked AI infrastructure.[5][INDUSTRIES] Rotation-state label: AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation.[1][5]

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 0.94 and QQQ 0.92 sit near neutral, with modestly positive skew (SPY +6.1%, QQQ +6.5%) signaling steady put demand but not panic-level hedging.[OPTIONS INTEL] SPY spot is close to its 750.0 gamma flip, implying dealer hedging can amplify index swings, while VIX 16.1 below its 20d MA 18.2 and MOVE 65.4 below its 20d 70.7 both indicate contained equity and bond vol despite the semiconductor drawdown.[VOL SIGNALS]

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar weakness (UUP -0.53%) alongside SLV +2.69% and GLD +2.03% shows a clear bid for precious metals as an alternative hedge while FX loosens.[CROSS-ASSET] Oil’s modest bounce (USO +0.69% with OVX 41.6 below its 20d 51.0) plus small credit gains in LQD +0.17% and HYG +0.15% confirm that today’s equity pain is localized to chips and growth, not a broad risk-off across commodities and credit.[CROSS-ASSET][VOL SIGNALS]

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 68.0/100 sits in Greed territory, and top individual movers were GDX +4.48% (Industries, gold miners), IHI +3.80% (Industries, medical devices), and SILJ +3.16% (Industries, junior silver), with XLV best 20d sector momentum at +11.84% and XLK worst at -8.89%.[FEAR & GREED][SECTORS][INDUSTRIES] KLAC vol 2.5x with price -11.51% signals strong distribution in The Fab 7 chip equipment, while MAGS 2.1x with -1.12% suggests targeted selling in industrial security names.[VOL SPIKES] Teradyne (TER) is worth watching after intraday volatility around a Susquehanna target hike and a wide high–low range tied to chip equipment spending expectations.[MOVER TER] Lam Research (LRCX) also deserves attention given its wide intraday range and conflicting price prints vs prior updates, hinting at unstable liquidity in front-line semi-cap names.[MOVER LRCX] GLD is a third watch: +2.03% today with ATM_IV 18.2% and PCR_vol 0.38 near its 377.0 gamma flip, making it a potential focal point for hedging flows if metals strength persists.[CROSS-ASSET][OPTIONS INTEL]
